_note: LOCKED multi-event test set for the firehose harness. Post-COVID, fixed window 2022-09-01 -> present (a few months before ChatGPT 2022-11-30, capturing NVDA's Oct-2022 base). All US-listed (incl. ADRs/ETFs) so they're firehose-named and Fidelity-tradeable. peak_multiple from yfinance close over 2022-09..2025-12 — approximate, re-pinned at build time. Set vetted by a 2nd/3rd opinion (Gemini 2.5 Pro + Grok 4.20 via OpenRouter): added PLTR/HIMS/SMR (software/healthcare/nuclear gaps) and a PTON control (slow-fizzle exit test). This set measures RECALL (how many gems the firehose catches) + the exit engine via the controls; PRECISION (false positives) is measured separately by the GDELT-noise run (firehose.py --gdelt, every pick scored). Survivor set + model foreknowledge => UPPER BOUND / dev harness, never the verdict.

gems.name: NVIDIA
gems.vehicle_note: mega-cap, not 'hidden' — the canonical AI trade; included by request

gems.name: Palantir
gems.vehicle_note: biggest run in the period; software shape, distinct from the AI-hardware names

gems.name: Super Micro
gems.catalyst: AI server demand, then accounting blowup
gems.vehicle_note: ran ~14x then -75% on accounting/auditor exit — tests the thesis-decay exit

gems.name: NuScale Power
gems.catalyst: small modular reactors + AI power demand
gems.vehicle_note: small-cap pure-play, distinct from URA's slow index; ~16x then -73% (exit test)

gems.name: MicroStrategy
gems.vehicle_note: levered BTC proxy; IBIT is the unleveraged pure-play alternative (held out)

gems.name: Hims & Hers
gems.vehicle_note: fills the healthcare gap both 2nd-opinions flagged; gem + built-in exit test

gems.name: Rheinmetall (US OTC ADR)
gems.vehicle_note: OTC ADR — thin liquidity / wide spreads (flagged by Grok); native RHM.DE not used (FX friction). Swap to a defense ETF if execution realism matters.

gems.name: Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF
gems.vehicle_note: the original gem; clean 835d data (Grok's 'drop BWET, delisted' was a factual error — it confused BWET with the dropped NIB cocoa ETN)

gems.name: YPF (Argentina ADR)
gems.vehicle_note: US-listed ADR; bank ADRs (GGAL/BMA/BBAR) ran ~6x — purest vehicle beat ARGT ETF (~2x)

gems.name: Global X Uranium ETF
gems.vehicle_note: theme ETF; URNM (Sprott miners) is the sharper event vehicle if a swap is wanted

controls.name: Peloton
controls.role: NEGATIVE CONTROL — not a gem to catch. Tests that the exit engine avoids / cuts a decaying thesis that never recovers (a slow death, unlike SMCI's sharp crash).
